$comment: ARM64 (AArch64) target descriptor (plan 13 AS3 + plan 12 D-ML7-2.1). 100% JSON-configured — the same target-blind LIR substrate that drives x86_64 reads this file. Adding ARM64 = drop this file; no C++ change. Encoding shape: `fixed32` (32-bit fixed word + bit-field slots). Calling convention: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa).

target.name: arm64
target.description: ARM64 AArch64 — minimal opcode set + AAPCS64 calling convention + fixed32 byte-encoder. Cycle-3 scope: ret (0xD65F03C0) + mov reg-reg via ORR alias (0xAA0003E0 base + Rd + Rm).

opcodes.$comment: Slot 0 = the Invalid sentinel — same discipline as x86_64. Substrate's addInst guard rejects opcode 0 loudly.
Virtual-ISA pseudo-op — same role as x86's `arg`: 'this LirReg is the function's k-th input parameter'. ML7 callconv lowering rewrites to the real mov-from-arg-reg sequence.

AAPCS64 has no native `mov reg, reg` instruction — the canonical encoding is `ORR Xd, XZR, Xm` (32-bit fixed word 0xAA0003E0 = base + Rn=XZR=31; Rd and Rm via slots). LIR's `mov` is 2-operand (result + 1 source); 3-address natively (no requires2Address).

wires.$comment: ORR Xd, XZR, Xm — bits 16..20 hold Rm (the source register's encoding). The fixed word's bits 9..5 = 0b11111 (XZR) are baked in; the wire only adds Rm bits.

opcodes.$comment: AAPCS64 RET (X30 implicit) = 0xD65F03C0. No operands at LIR level; the link register x30 is implicit per the calling convention.
Hard-trap. AArch64 BRK #0 = 0xD4200000. Cycle-3 includes for completeness so a synthesized LIR can terminate without depending on `ret` semantics.

$registersComment: AAPCS64 general-purpose registers x0..x30 + zero-register XZR (encoded as 31 in source operands; uses register's hwEncoding). Stack pointer `sp` is also encoded as 31 but in the SP-slot context — hwEncoding=31 with class GPR is shared between XZR and SP at the byte level. NEON/FP regs v0..v31 use the same 5-bit field but in a separate class. Cycle-3 declares enough registers for AAPCS64 callconv + the encoded mov/ret/brk subset.

registers.$comment: Stack pointer shares hwEncoding=31 with XZR at the byte level (the context determines which: SP-using addressing modes vs ZR-encoding instructions). The substrate treats them as distinct names with distinct ordinals; the JSON-side ordinal is what matters for the cc lookup.
registers.name: sp

$callingConventionsComment: AAPCS64 (https://github.com/ARM-software/abi-aa/blob/main/aapcs64/aapcs64.rst). Integer args: x0..x7. FP args: v0..v7 — omitted in cycle-3 (no FP support yet at the asm-tier). Caller-saved: x0..x18 (incl. x16/x17 = IP0/IP1; PLT-clobberable). Callee-saved: x19..x28 + x29 (frame ptr) + x30 (link reg).
